tick_over_tick_delta_vs_1000.interpretation: FIRST POST-AUBANK RE-DEPLOYMENT INITIATED. Strategy submitted a single new BUY (TATAPOWER 110 @ ₹443 = ₹48,730 = 4.87% of capital) at 10:13:02 — 41 minutes after the AUBANK exit. The choice is structurally clean: (i) Power sector, NOT a BFSI re-stack (Trigger C does not fire), (ii) sized at 4.87% — well under the 0.15 per-name cap, (iii) projected deployment 54.07% on fill — still 15.93 pp under the 0.70 cap, (iv) no duplicate-order or near-duplicate pattern (TATAPOWER is a fresh name, not a re-add of an exited name). This is the disciplined SELL→BUY recycle the override stack was designed to enable, not punish. Power cluster on full fill = NTPC (5.06%) + TATAPOWER (4.87%) = 9.93% — comfortably below the 30% sector cap and below the 16% level that would echo the prior 3-name Power cluster from Day-1.

findings: TATAPOWER OPEN BUY (110 @ ₹443 = 4.87% of capital): single, modestly-sized Power admission consuming roughly half the cap-implied per-name slot (4.87% vs 15% cap) and one-fifth of the cap-implied Power-sector slot (4.87% vs 30% sector cap inclusive of NTPC). No quality concerns flagged at the override layer — projected post-fill deployment 54.07% has 15.93 pp of headroom to the 0.70 cap, and Power-sector exposure post-fill 9.93% has 20.07 pp of headroom to the 0.30 cap. Trigger F (complacency watch from 10:00) DOES NOT fire: spec required 3+ new BUYs in a 60-min window taking deployment back above 65%; observed pattern is 1 BUY in 17 min and projected deployment is 54.07%.

BFSI sector concentration: 17.65% (₹176,520 — MAHABANK 9.78% + PNBHOUSING 7.87%). 12.35 pp BUFFER below the 0.30 max_sector_pct override. Trigger C (BFSI re-stacking watch from 10:00) DOES NOT fire: TATAPOWER is Power, not BFSI; no new BFSI BUY has been attempted since the AUBANK exit. The cap continues to function as a forward back-pressure ring against BFSI re-stacking, currently dormant.

MAHABANK at LTP ₹79.48 / pnl_pct -2.48% (snapshot 35 at 10:13:16). Trigger G (MAHABANK extension watch from 10:00) DOES NOT fire: MAHABANK is 3.52 pp ABOVE the -6.00% fire line (₹76.61) and has materially recovered from its prior-session pain regime (-5.31% on Apr-23, -6.42% on Apr-24, now -2.48%). The single-name MTM drag has compressed from -₹6,276 (Apr-24) to -₹2,424 (today). The largest single-name LOSER today is now NESTLEIND no it's actually MAHABANK at -₹2,424 — but PNBHOUSING (+₹5,156), NESTLEIND (+₹1,289), GRANULES (+₹1,845), and NTPC (+₹531) more than offset, leaving the book at +₹5,807 unrealized.

Largest single-name (filled): NESTLEIND 12.00% (₹120,099.90) — 3.00 pp BUFFER below the 0.15 per-name cap. On TATAPOWER fill, the hierarchy is NESTLEIND 12.00% → MAHABANK 9.78% → PNBHOUSING 7.87% → NTPC 5.06% → GOLDBEES 5.00% → TATAPOWER 4.87% → GRANULES 4.77% → HINDUNILVR 4.74%. Concentration index (sum-of-squares of weights) is structurally improved post-AUBANK and remains so on the TATAPOWER add — no name is dominating top-of-book risk.

Deployment: 49.21% (filled book) / 54.07% (on TATAPOWER fill). 20.79 pp / 15.93 pp BUFFER below the 0.70 max_deployed_pct cap. The cap is non-binding by a comfortable margin in either scenario. Admission surface remains open for additional quality entries up to ~₹207,724 / ~₹158,994 of incremental headroom.

Cash buffer: ₹508,227.18 (50.50% of book by LTP; 50.79% by cost-basis). On TATAPOWER fill, cash drops to ₹459,497 (45.66% by LTP). Either way this remains the largest cash reserve since Day-1 inception and is well above any historical session reading. Sufficient absorbance for ~5% of book MTM drawdown without forced liquidation, and ample opportunistic-add capacity.

Daily P&L: realized -₹865.92 (AUBANK exit only); intraday MTM unrealized +₹5,807; book vs Apr-24 EOD +₹2,754.73 (+0.27%). The book is GREEN today. Buffer to base 3% loss gate from realized loss: ₹29,150 (97% of full budget). Buffer to internal -1% findings trigger (Trigger A from 10:00): ₹9,140. All P&L-based loss gates remain dormant by a wide margin.

FMCG concentration: 16.75% (NESTLEIND 12.00% + HINDUNILVR 4.74%). Unchanged tick-over-tick. HUL Q4 FY26 results scheduled in week of Apr-28 remain a secondary binary event in book within 5 sessions, but bounded at HINDUNILVR's 4.74% — far smaller than the AUBANK 18.44% binary-event lot that was successfully neutralized this morning. No pre-emptive action needed; rule-11 (binary-event discipline) will continue to govern.

Strategy SELL-then-BUY recycle pattern is now observable in this session: 09:31:56 AUBANK SELL → 10:13:02 TATAPOWER BUY. The 41-minute gap between the trim and the re-deployment is reasonable (not instant churn, not paralysis). The new entry is in a different sector (Power vs BFSI), respects per-name discipline (4.87% vs 12% prior largest), and consumes only a fraction of the freed capital (₹48,730 of the ₹183,767 inflow). This is a structurally healthy re-deployment cadence — reward, do not penalize.

Composite distress meta-trigger (Trigger D from 10:00): all three legs DORMANT. (i) book P&L at +0.27% (well above -1.5% threshold), (ii) no single-name at ≤-7% (worst is MAHABANK -2.48%), (iii) strategy not silent (most recent action was TATAPOWER BUY at 10:13:02, ~17 min ago at assessment time). Risk-level is firmly LOW; no path to elevation visible on current trajectory.
actions_taken: NO numeric override changes this tick. Disciplined hold consistent with the established surgical-fire pattern. The book has just executed a textbook SELL→BUY recycle (AUBANK exit → TATAPOWER add), and tightening overrides now would punish the strategy layer for the very behavior that the prior 5 sessions of override-stacking were designed to enable. None of the 7 pre-armed triggers from 10:00 (A, B-retired, C, D, E, F, G) has fired — every one remains dormant.

All seven existing overrides held: min_confidence=0.60, max_position_pct=0.15, max_deployed_pct=0.70, default_sl_pct=0.015, min_sl_pct=0.01, max_sl_pct=0.04, max_sector_pct=0.30. Each remains a forward back-pressure ring against future re-stacking — non-binding on current exposure but binding on hypothetical re-admissions. Specifically: (i) the 0.30 sector cap continues to gate any incremental BFSI BUY ~₹123K beyond current exposure, (ii) the 0.15 per-name cap continues to gate any single name at ~₹150K, (iii) the 0.70 deployment cap continues to gate cumulative deployment at ~₹158K of headroom on TATAPOWER fill, (iv) the SL band [1.0%, 4.0%] continues to gate per-name new-entry risk at ≤0.60% of book.

Risk level HELD at LOW (unchanged from 10:00). Structural drivers remain: (i) AUBANK binary exposure resolved (zero), (ii) BFSI 17.65% < 30% cap (12.35 pp buffer), (iii) no per-name breach (largest 12.00% NESTLEIND, 3.00 pp buffer to cap), (iv) deployment 49.21% / 54.07% on fill < 70% cap (15.93 pp buffer), (v) book P&L positive (+₹2,754.73 / +0.27% MTM), (vi) cash buffer 50.50% (highest since inception). No leg of the LOW→MEDIUM→HIGH→CRITICAL ladder has any firing path on current trajectory.
pre_armed_triggers_intraday: TRIGGER A (PnL-based, primary, CARRIED FROM 10:00): If book daily P&L ≤ -1.00% (₹-10,006) at any tick → FIRE daily_loss_limit_pct 0.03 → 0.025. Currently at +0.27% MTM. Buffer ₹12,761 to fire (book MTM has IMPROVED, widening the buffer).

TRIGGER B (RETIRED for AUBANK, CARRIED FROM 10:00): The AUBANK adverse post-event trigger is no longer applicable — position is closed. Pre-arm rolls forward as a generic 'next BFSI binary-event trigger' if a new BFSI position is admitted (none currently in flight; TATAPOWER is Power).

TRIGGER C (BFSI re-stacking, CARRIED FROM 10:00): If strategy admits a NEW BFSI BUY within 60 min of next assessment that pushes BFSI above 25% via mark + entry → FIRE max_sector_pct 0.30 → 0.25. Currently dormant (TATAPOWER is Power; BFSI flat at 17.65%).

TRIGGER D (composite HIGH→CRITICAL meta, CARRIED FROM 10:00): Generic distress meta requires (i) book P&L ≤ -1.5% AND (ii) any single-name ≤ -7% AND (iii) strategy silent ≥60 min after first sub-threshold breach → ELEVATE risk_level CRITICAL + FIRE daily_loss_limit_pct 0.03 → 0.020 + max_position_pct 0.15 → 0.12. All three legs DORMANT.

TRIGGER F (complacency watch, CARRIED FROM 10:00, NEAR-FIRING-CONDITION-RECEDED): If strategy admits 3+ new BUYs in a 60-min window taking deployment back above 65% → flag in next risk_assessment as a re-stacking pattern. Current count: 1 BUY (TATAPOWER) in 17 minutes; projected deployment 54.07% on fill (10.93 pp under the 65% threshold). Ratchet 1 of 3 — material headroom remains.

TRIGGER G (MAHABANK extension watch, CARRIED FROM 10:00): If MAHABANK marks below ₹76.61 (pnl_pct ≤ -6.00% on the 1200-share lot) → re-assess whether the 0.04 max_sl_pct ceiling is too loose for the next admission queue (potential lever: 0.04 → 0.035). Currently at ₹79.48 / -2.48%, 3.52 pp BUFFER. The earlier session pain trade has materially decompressed.

TRIGGER H (NEW, TATAPOWER-fill-pending watch): If TATAPOWER fills above ₹447 (>0.9% above order price ₹443) → flag as a slippage-on-fill that the strategy layer should observe in the next strategy_review (NOT a numeric tightening trigger; an observation feed). TRIGGER I (NEW, Power-cluster re-emergence watch): If a SECOND new Power name (beyond TATAPOWER + NTPC) is admitted today taking Power-sector exposure above 13% → FIRE max_sector_pct 0.30 → 0.25 (Power-cluster equivalent of Trigger C for BFSI). Day-1 demonstrated that 3-name Power clusters can build quickly (TORNTPOWER + NTPC + ADANIPOWER); 2 names today is fine, 3 should re-engage the cap. Currently 2 names projected (NTPC + TATAPOWER on fill), 1 name filled — trigger is forward-armed.
